title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Hialeah
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Hialeah, FL
heroParagraph: Hialeah's sun beats down hard on pool decks, from Hialeah Park to Palm Springs North, and afternoon storms bring mosquitoes fast. A custom aluminum pool screen enclosure keeps your pool shaded, bug-free, and swim-ready — engineered to Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ wind standard.
heroImageAlt: Custom aluminum pool screen enclosure with a mansard roof over a backyard pool in Hialeah, FL
introHeading: Built for Hialeah's Heat, Engineered for Miami-Dade's Wind Code
valueParagraphs: Hialeah doesn't have brisa marina an ocean breeze to soften the afternoon sun: from the block homes lining Palm Springs North to the ranch houses near Hialeah Park, backyard pool decks bake all summer. A screen-roof enclosure changes that instantly, filtering harsh UV while keeping air moving so the space stays cool enough to actually use. Choose a mansard or dome roofline to match your home's profile, and the aluminum frame won't fade, rust, or warp under year-round South Florida sun.

Between the canals running through West Hialeah and the standing water after summer storms, mosquitoes and no-see-ums find their way into every uncovered pool. A full aluminum cage seals them out along with leaves, frogs, and lizards, so the water — and your pool chemicals — stay cleaner with far less scrubbing. For Hialeah's close-knit, multi-generational households, the mesh walls also double as a real safety barrier, keeping toddlers and pets out of the water when nobody's watching.

Every enclosure we build for Hialeah homeowners is engineered to Miami-Dade's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone standard — 175 mph-rated aluminum framing backed by Miami-Dade NOA product approval and sealed engineering drawings. Permits route through the City of Hialeah Building Department, and our team pulls and manages that paperwork for you from design to final inspection. We handle everything in-house, from free 3D design to installation, with financing available so a properly engineered pool cage fits your budget, not just your backyard.
permitNote: Hialeah pool enclosures fall under Miami-Dade's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone code. We provide sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA-approved aluminum products rated to 175 mph winds, then pull your permit through the City of Hialeah Building Department so you don't have to.
localProof: Licensed and insured with 15+ years serving Miami-Dade families and 500+ completed projects, our in-house crew has built pool cages throughout Hialeah, from Hialeah Park to Palm Springs North, using marine-grade powder-coated aluminum that holds up to South Florida's heat and storms.

answerAnswer: Pricing depends on cage size, roof style (flat, dome, mansard, or gable), and screen mesh — standard, super-screen, or pet/no-see-um mesh. Most Hialeah projects fall within {range}, calculated at {perSqft}. Every quote includes a free in-house 3D design and covers Miami-Dade HVHZ engineering and City of Hialeah permitting, so pricing reflects a fully code-compliant structure.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit for a pool enclosure in Hialeah?
faqs.answer: Yes. Any permanent aluminum pool enclosure in Hialeah requires a building permit, sealed engineering, and Miami-Dade NOA product approval rated to 175 mph winds under the county's HVHZ code. Permits are processed through the City of Hialeah Building Department, and we handle the entire application and inspection process for you.

faqs.question: What screen mesh options are available for a Hialeah pool cage?
faqs.answer: We offer standard 18x14 fiberglass screen for everyday sun and bug protection, super-screen or no-see-um mesh for finer pest control near canals and standing water, and pet-resistant screen for homes with dogs or cats. Each option balances airflow, visibility, and durability differently — we'll help you choose based on your yard.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen an existing pool cage instead of building a new one?
faqs.answer: If your aluminum frame is structurally sound, we can rescreen it with fresh mesh — often a faster, lower-cost fix for storm-damaged or sun-worn panels. If the frame is outdated, undersized, or doesn't meet current HVHZ wind ratings, a new engineered enclosure is usually the better long-term investment.

faqs.question: Does a pool enclosure meet Florida's pool safety barrier requirements?
faqs.answer: A properly designed and permitted aluminum cage with secure, self-latching screen doors can serve as a pool safety barrier under Florida's residential swimming pool safety act. We design door placement and hardware to meet code, giving Hialeah families an added layer of protection for kids and pets.

title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Coral Gables
h1: Custom Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Coral Gables, FL
heroParagraph: From Gables Estates to Ponce-Davis, a custom aluminum pool cage keeps your Coral Gables pool free of mosquitoes, leaves and debris while cutting UV exposure — engineered to Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ standard and finished to match Coral Gables' Mediterranean Revival architecture.
heroImageAlt: Custom aluminum pool cage enclosure over a backyard pool in Coral Gables, Florida, with a screen roof and powder-coated marine-grade frame
introHeading: Built for Coral Gables Pools, Inside and Out
valueParagraphs: Waterfront pools along Old Cutler Road, in Gables Estates and around Cocoplum sit close to Biscayne Bay, where salt-laden air corrodes ordinary screen frames within a few seasons. Our pool enclosures use powder-coated marine-grade aluminum that resists pitting and discoloration, paired with super-screen or no-see-um mesh that keeps sand fleas and lovebugs off the deck. The cage also blocks storm debris carried in off the water, so cleanup after a squall off the bay takes minutes, not hours.

Move inland toward Ponce-Davis and Hammock Oaks and the challenge shifts from salt air to relentless Florida sun — a screen roof over the pool cuts direct UV significantly while keeping the space breezy, not boxed in. Because Coral Gables enforces citywide Board of Architects review for exterior additions on top of any HOA guidelines, we design every enclosure — mansard, dome, gable or flat roofline — to read as a natural extension of the home's Mediterranean Revival or Spanish-style architecture, not a bolt-on cage.

Every enclosure we install in Coral Gables is engineered to Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ wind standard, carries Miami-Dade NOA product approval, and is permitted through the City of Coral Gables Building Division before our in-house crew begins work — permits included, no runaround. The mesh also forms a real barrier around the water for kids and pets, and cuts the leaves and pollen that drive up chemical and filtration costs. valueParagraphsEs: Las piscinas frente al agua a lo largo de Old Cutler Road, en Gables Estates y alrededor de Cocoplum están cerca de la Bahía de Biscayne, donde el aire salino corroe los marcos de malla comunes en pocas temporadas. Nuestros cerramientos usan aluminio grado marino con recubrimiento en polvo que resiste la corrosión y la decoloración, junto con malla super-screen o no-see-um que mantiene fuera pulgas de arena y jejenes. La jaula también bloquea los escombros que trae el viento desde la bahía, así que la limpieza después de una tormenta toma minutos, no horas.

Al avanzar hacia el interior, cerca de Ponce-Davis y Hammock Oaks, el reto cambia del aire salino al sol intenso de Florida — un techo de malla sobre la piscina reduce notablemente la radiación UV directa sin encerrar el espacio. Como Coral Gables exige revisión de la Junta de Arquitectos de la ciudad para adiciones exteriores, además de cualquier normativa de HOA, diseñamos cada cerramiento — mansarda, domo, a dos aguas o techo plano — para que luzca como una extensión natural de la arquitectura Mediterranean Revival o de estilo español de la vivienda, no como una jaula añadida.

Todo cerramiento que instalamos en Coral Gables está diseñado según el estándar HVHZ de 175 mph de Miami-Dade, cuenta con aprobación de producto NOA de Miami-Dade, y se permisa a través de la División de Construcción de la Ciudad de Coral Gables antes de que nuestro equipo interno inicie el trabajo — permisos incluidos, sin complicaciones. La malla también forma una barrera real alrededor del agua para niños y mascotas, y reduce las hojas y el polen que elevan los costos de químicos y filtración. Respaldamos cada proyecto con diseño 3D gratuito, opciones de financiamiento, y más de 15 años con más de 500 instalaciones en el sur de Florida.

Como Coral Gables también tiene su propia Junta de Arquitectos para revisión de diseño, preparamos los renders con anticipación para que su cerramiento pase tanto la revisión estructural como la estética a la primera — nosotros gestionamos cada trámite por usted.

faqsEs.answer: Sí. Miami-Dade es una Zona de Huracanes de Alta Velocidad (HVHZ), así que cualquier cerramiento de piscina de aluminio permanente necesita un permiso de construcción con ingeniería sellada y aprobación de producto NOA de Miami-Dade, diseñado para vientos de 175 mph. title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Homestead
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Homestead, FL
heroParagraph: Homestead's sun doesn't let up — and neither do the mosquitoes drifting in from the Redland's canals and groves. A custom aluminum pool screen enclosure keeps your pool clean, shaded, and bug-free, engineered to Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ standard. Free 3D design included.
heroImageAlt: Aluminum pool screen enclosure over a backyard pool in Homestead, FL
introHeading: Custom Pool Screen Enclosures Built for Homestead's Heat and Bugs
valueParagraphs: Homestead summers push heat index well past 100°F, and an unscreened pool deck turns into a griddle by midafternoon. Our aluminum screen enclosures cut direct UV load while keeping airflow open, so families in Keys Gate and other planned communities can use the pool from morning through dusk instead of just sunrise and sunset. Powder-coated marine-grade aluminum framing holds its color under that South Dade sun, and every design clears HOA architectural review before we submit for permit.

Homestead backs up to the Redland's nurseries, groves, and canal banks, which means no-see-ums, mosquitoes, love bugs, and wind-blown leaves find their way into an open pool fast. A screened cage seals that out with 18/14 standard mesh or upgraded no-see-um screen for properties closer to the ag fields, keeping the water and deck cleaner between services. Less debris means less chlorine, less scrubbing, and a pump that isn't fighting organic buildup every week.

Every enclosure we build in Homestead is sealed by a Florida engineer and permitted through the City of Homestead's building department, with Miami-Dade NOA product approval behind the aluminum and screen system to the required 175 mph HVHZ standard. The frame also doubles as a safety barrier around the pool, an extra layer parents in family-dense neighborhoods like Waterstone and downtown Homestead count on. Financing is available, and our in-house crew handles the paperwork end to end.
permitNote: Homestead pool enclosures require a building permit with sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA product approval to the 175 mph HVHZ standard; we handle the City of Homestead building department submission for you.

HVHZ engineering, Miami-Dade NOA-approved materials, and City of Homestead permitting are included in every quote. Free 3D design shows the final layout before you sign.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to build a pool enclosure in Homestead?
faqs.answer: Yes. Miami-Dade is a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, so any permanent aluminum pool enclosure needs a permit with sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA product approval, rated for 175 mph winds. In Homestead, that permit is filed through the City of Homestead's building department rather than county RER, and our team handles the full submission.

faqs.question: What screen mesh works best for a Homestead pool cage?
faqs.answer: Standard 18/14 fiberglass screen stops most leaves and insects and is the most economical choice. Properties near the Redland's groves and canals often upgrade to no-see-um mesh to block the small biting gnats that standard screen lets through, and pet owners frequently choose reinforced super-screen or pet-screen for extra durability.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen my existing pool cage instead of building a new one?
faqs.answer: If your aluminum frame is sound and simply worn, torn, or discolored, rescreening is usually faster and less costly than a full rebuild. We inspect the frame first — if the structure has corrosion, storm damage, or doesn't meet current HVHZ standards, we'll walk you through repair versus replacement before any work starts.

faqs.question: Does a pool enclosure meet Florida's pool safety barrier requirements?
faqs.answer: A properly built and latched aluminum pool enclosure with self-closing screen doors satisfies Florida's pool safety barrier code, adding a physical layer between the pool and the yard for kids and pets. We design door hardware and access points to meet code as part of every Homestead project.

title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Miami
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Miami, FL
heroParagraph: From Coconut Grove's bay air to Coral Way's relentless sun, Miami pools need real protection. Our custom aluminum pool enclosures, engineered to Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ standard, keep bugs, leaves and UV out while your pool stays open, breezy and easy to maintain.
heroImageAlt: Custom aluminum pool enclosure with screened dome roof over a backyard pool in Miami, FL
introHeading: Built for Miami-Dade's HVHZ — and Your Backyard
valueParagraphs: Homes near Biscayne Bay in Coconut Grove and Morningside sit close enough to salt air that standard fasteners and untreated frames corrode fast. We build every pool enclosure from powder-coated marine-grade aluminum, engineered to Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ wind rating, so the structure holds up against salt spray, tropical storms and the humidity that comes off the water. You get a screened pool cage that shrugs off the coastal air instead of pitting within a few seasons.

Farther inland in neighborhoods like Coral Way, the issue isn't salt — it's Miami's relentless sun and mosquito season. A screen roof cuts direct UV significantly while keeping the pool deck breezy instead of trapping heat like a solid patio cover. Super-screen and no-see-um mesh options keep out the gnats and no-see-ums that swarm at dusk, and the enclosed deck means far fewer leaves, frogs and debris landing in the water — which cuts down on the pool cleaning and chemicals you'd otherwise need every week.

Because Miami-Dade is a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, a permanent pool cage isn't a weekend add-on — it requires a building permit, sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA product approval before it goes up, filed through Miami-Dade RER or your local building department. We handle every step of that process in-house, from the free 3D design and permit drawings to installation with our own crew, and we offer financing so a fully code-compliant enclosure fits your budget without the paperwork headache.
permitNote: All Miami pool enclosures require a permit, sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA product approval rated to 175 mph winds — filed through Miami-Dade RER or the City of Miami building department. We prepare and file the full package for you.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit for a pool enclosure in Miami?
faqs.answer: Yes. Miami-Dade is a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, so any permanent aluminum pool cage requires a building permit, sealed structural engineering and Miami-Dade NOA product approval rated to 175 mph winds. Permits are filed through Miami-Dade RER or the City of Miami building department — we handle the entire filing for you.

faqs.question: What screen options are available for a Miami pool cage?
faqs.answer: We install standard 18x14 screen, super-screen for stronger wind and debris resistance, and no-see-um mesh that blocks the tiny biting gnats common near Miami's canals and bay-adjacent yards. Pet-screen is also available for households with dogs or cats that spend time on the pool deck.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen my existing pool cage, or do I need a new one?
faqs.answer: If your aluminum frame is structurally sound, we can rescreen it with upgraded mesh. But many older Miami pool cages predate current Miami-Dade HVHZ wind standards — if the frame shows corrosion or wasn't engineered to today's 175 mph code, we'll recommend a fully permitted replacement instead.

faqs.question: Does a pool enclosure meet Florida's pool safety barrier requirements?
faqs.answer: A properly permitted aluminum pool enclosure with self-latching screen doors can serve as a code-compliant safety barrier under Florida's pool safety act, helping keep young children and pets from unsupervised pool access. We confirm door hardware and barrier details meet code during your permit review.

Near Cape Florida and the Ocean Club, backyard pools sit under intense, unfiltered island sun for most of the year — a screen roof cuts UV and glare while keeping the space open and shaded, not boxed in. A full enclosure also works as a safety barrier around the pool for kids and pets, and because leaves, frogs and debris stay out, you'll spend far less time skimming and dosing chemicals.

Because Key Biscayne sits in Miami-Dade's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, every enclosure we build — from Fernwood to Harbor Drive — is engineered to 175 mph wind loads with Miami-Dade NOA product approval and a sealed permit set, filed through the Village of Key Biscayne Building Department or Miami-Dade RER. introHeadingEs: Diseñado para el Aire Salino y el Sol de Miami Beach
valueParagraphsEs: Miami Beach es una isla de barrera — cada casa está a pocas cuadras del Atlántico o de la Bahía de Biscayne, y ese aire cargado de sal desgasta rápido el metal sin protección. Desde South of Fifth hasta las propiedades frente al agua en Sunset Islands y La Gorce Island, construimos jaulas de piscina exclusivamente con aluminio marino con recubrimiento en polvo, la misma especificación que se usa en muros de contención y herrajes de muelles, para que su estructura resista la picadura y la corrosión en lugar de opacarse en un par de temporadas.

Un cerramiento de piscina con malla le permite dejar las puertas corredizas abiertas durante todo el verano en Miami Beach sin que entren mosquitos, jejenes (no-see-ums) o restos de palmeras al agua. Reduce la pérdida de cloro y el tiempo de limpieza, bloquea la intensa radiación UV que refleja la bahía, y le da a los niños y mascotas una barrera real alrededor de la piscina. Elija malla estándar 18/14, malla no-see-um para las noches con más insectos, o super-screen resistente a mascotas, bajo un techo de estilo plano, a dos aguas, domo o mansarda que combine con las líneas de su casa.

Como Miami Beach está dentro de la Zona de Huracanes de Alta Velocidad (HVHZ) del condado Miami-Dade, un cerramiento permanente de piscina no es un proyecto de fin de semana — requiere un paquete de ingeniería sellado, aprobación de producto NOA de Miami-Dade, y un permiso revisado por el Departamento de Construcción de la Ciudad de Miami Beach antes de instalar un solo poste en su terraza. Nuestras estructuras de aluminio están certificadas para 175 mph, y manejamos todo el proceso de permisos internamente, desde los planos hasta la inspección final, para que el papeleo nunca llegue a su escritorio.

title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Sunny Isles Beach
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Sunny Isles Beach, FL
heroParagraph: Sunny Isles Beach sits on a barrier island between the Atlantic and the Intracoastal, which means every outdoor structure fights salt air from two directions. Our marine-grade aluminum pool enclosures keep your pool shaded, bug-free, and protected — without corroding.
heroImageAlt: Aluminum pool screen enclosure over a backyard pool in Sunny Isles Beach, FL
introHeading: Pool Enclosures Engineered for Sunny Isles Beach's Salt Air
valueParagraphs: Homes in Golden Shores and along the canals off Maule Lake sit within a few hundred yards of open water on both sides — the Atlantic to the east, the Intracoastal to the west. That double dose of salt air corrodes standard aluminum and steel fast. We build every pool cage from powder-coated marine-grade aluminum, engineered to Miami-Dade's 175 mph HVHZ wind standard and approved under Miami-Dade NOA, so the frame holds up to salt spray and storm season alike.

A screened enclosure turns a Sunny Isles Beach pool deck into usable living space again — no mosquitoes drifting in from the mangrove-lined canals around Maule Lake, no palm debris or no-see-ums fouling the water, and noticeably less time spent skimming and balancing chemicals. Standard 18x14 mesh handles typical flying insects; step up to super-screen or pet-resistant mesh where no-see-ums or a dog are a factor. The enclosure also functions as a recognized barrier layer that supports Florida's pool child-safety code.

Because Sunny Isles Beach is its own incorporated city, your permit runs through the Sunny Isles Beach Building Department rather than unincorporated Miami-Dade RER — we handle that submittal, the sealed engineering, and the Miami-Dade NOA paperwork so you're not chasing forms. Our in-house crew installs on tight lots common in Golden Shores and along the Intracoastal, with mansard, dome, gable, or flat screen-roof styles to match the home. Every project starts with a free 3D design, and financing is available.
permitNote: Sunny Isles Beach is a fully incorporated city, so pool enclosure permits are filed with the Sunny Isles Beach Building Department, not Miami-Dade RER directly. Because the city sits in the HVHZ, every enclosure still requires sealed structural engineering and a product approved under Miami-Dade NOA, rated to 175 mph winds — we prepare and submit that full package for you.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit for a pool enclosure in Sunny Isles Beach?
faqs.answer: Yes. Any permanent aluminum pool enclosure requires a building permit, sealed engineering, and product approval under Miami-Dade NOA, since the city sits in the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone and structures must be rated to 175 mph winds. Because Sunny Isles Beach is incorporated, the permit is filed with the city's own building department rather than Miami-Dade RER — we prepare and submit the full package.

faqs.question: What screen mesh holds up best on a barrier island like Sunny Isles Beach?
faqs.answer: Standard 18x14 mesh works fine for general bug control, but with open water on both the Atlantic and Intracoastal sides, no-see-ums are more persistent here than inland. Most Sunny Isles Beach homeowners upgrade to super-screen or pet-resistant mesh, and we always spec marine-grade powder-coated aluminum framing so salt spray doesn't pit or corrode the structure over time.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen an existing pool cage instead of building a new one?
faqs.answer: Often, yes. If the aluminum frame is sound and up to code, we can pull the old screen and rescreen with a heavier or no-see-um mesh for a fraction of the cost of a new enclosure. If salt air has weakened the frame or it predates current HVHZ standards, we'll recommend a full rebuild instead — our free 3D design visit tells you which situation you're in.

faqs.question: Does a screen enclosure meet Florida's pool child-safety barrier code?
faqs.answer: A properly latched, self-closing screen enclosure with locking doors can serve as a recognized barrier layer under Florida's pool safety requirements, in addition to protecting swimmers from sun and insects. We build in code-compliant screen doors with self-closing hardware so the enclosure works as both a living-space upgrade and a safety layer for kids and pets.

valueParagraphsEs: El icónico dosel de robles y ficus de Pinecrest, a lo largo de Old Cutler Road y en The Bluffs, regala una sombra hermosa — y un flujo constante de mosquitos, jejenes, hojas y ranas arborícolas que terminan en la piscina. Un cerramiento de aluminio con malla sella tu piscina y su deck contra todo eso, mientras reduce el sol intenso típico del interior de Pinecrest, lejos de la brisa del mar. El resultado: una piscina más fresca y limpia, menos químicos y limpieza, y una verdadera barrera de seguridad para niños y mascotas.

Como Pinecrest es un municipio incorporado dentro de la Zona de Huracanes de Alta Velocidad (HVHZ) de Miami-Dade, un cerramiento permanente de piscina requiere permiso de construcción, ingeniería estructural sellada y aprobación NOA de Miami-Dade — revisada por el departamento de construcción del Village of Pinecrest, no solo por el condado. En lotes tipo estate con revisión arquitectónica, también importan detalles de techo y retiros. En AB Aluminum & Screens diseñamos cada estructura para resistir vientos de 175 mph y gestionamos todo el trámite de permisos, planos de ingeniería e inspecciones, para que tú no tengas que lidiar con el papeleo del Village ni del condado.

La mayoría de los propietarios en Pinecrest eligen un techo tipo mansarda o a dos aguas para combinar con la arquitectura mediterránea de estilo estate cerca de Old Cutler Road y Pinecrest Gardens, aunque los techos planos o tipo domo también funcionan bien. Como la densa vegetación trae más insectos, solemos recomendar malla no-see-um o super-screen en lugar de la malla estándar 18x14, más una puerta con malla. Cada estructura es de aluminio marino con recubrimiento en polvo, fabricada por nuestro equipo interno — con diseño 3D gratis y financiamiento disponible, respaldados por más de 15 años y 500+ proyectos en el sur de la Florida.

Our pool enclosures use powder-coated marine-grade aluminum framing built to resist that corrosion, paired with super-screen or standard 18x14 mesh that keeps salt-laden debris, palm fronds and no-see-ums out of the water without blocking the waterfront breeze homeowners buy into The Cove for in the first place.

Further west, Century Village and the Deer Creek golf community face a different enemy: relentless South Florida sun that fades vinyl and bakes an unscreened pool deck by early afternoon. A screen-roof enclosure — mansard, dome or flat — cuts glare and UV while keeping the pool area open and breezy. Both communities run active architectural review boards, and our design team submits elevations and HOA packages alongside your permit set so approval doesn't stall your project.

Every enclosure we build in Deerfield Beach is engineered to the 175 mph HVHZ standard required in Broward County, with sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval documentation submitted to the City of Deerfield Beach Building Department before we touch aluminum. Beyond wind resistance, a screened cage functions as a code-recognized barrier around the water, keeps kids and pets from wandering in unsupervised, and cuts the leaves and bugs that drive up chemical and cleaning costs.

valueParagraphs: Coral Springs backyards along the city's canal network — in neighborhoods like Eagle Trace, Turtle Run and Ramblewood — sit close to water and green space, which means mosquitoes, no-see-ums, palm debris and the occasional frog find their way to the pool deck fast. A custom aluminum screen enclosure seals that gap completely, keeping bugs and yard debris out while cutting the skimming, brushing and chemical dosing that canal-adjacent pools tend to need. It also adds a code-relevant barrier between the water and young kids or pets.

Coral Springs sits well inland, so the driving factor here isn't salt air — it's Florida sun. Afternoon UV across neighborhoods like Coral Springs Country Club and The Township is intense enough to make an unscreened deck unusable by 2 p.m. most of the year. Our powder-coated marine-grade aluminum frames diffuse that glare while our super-screen and no-see-um mesh options keep the breeze moving. Many Coral Springs HOAs also require architectural review before any pool structure goes up — our design team handles that submittal alongside your building permit.

Broward County is a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, and Coral Springs' Building Division enforces it strictly: any permanent aluminum enclosure needs sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval rated to 175 mph before a permit is issued. We handle that entire submittal in-house, from stamped drawings through final inspection, and build the frame to match — mansard, dome, gable or flat screen-roof styles, with screen doors sized to your existing pool deck. Free 3D design and financing are available before you commit.

Nuestros cerramientos usan aluminio marino recubierto en polvo, resistente a esa exposición, para que tu jaula de piscina conserve su acabado sin oxidarse ni descolorarse. Combinado con estructuras HVHZ certificadas para 175 mph, está diseñado para el viento y la sal a pocas cuadras del mar, no para una especificación genérica de tierra adentro.

Más hacia el interior, en zonas como Emerald Hills, Hollywood Lakes y Hollywood Hills, el reto cambia de la sal al sol y a las normas locales. El fuerte sol del sur de Florida desgasta los muebles y calienta el deck; un techo tipo screen reduce ese resplandor y mantiene el espacio ventilado. Muchas de estas comunidades exigen revisión de la HOA, así que gestionamos color y medidas junto con el permiso de Broward o la ciudad de Hollywood, para que tu proyecto avance sin rechazos.

Un cerramiento de piscina hace más que verse bien en el estilo de vida al aire libre de Hollywood: es una barrera física entre el agua y los niños pequeños o mascotas, y mantiene fuera mosquitos, jejenes, ranas arborícolas y escombros de tormenta. Elige malla estándar 18x14, malla no-see-um de tejido cerrado, o super screen resistente a mascotas, con techos tipo mansard, domo, gable o plano, y una puerta con mosquitero para el acceso diario. Menos escombros y sol también significan menos químicos y menos días de limpieza.

title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Hallandale Beach
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Hallandale Beach, FL
heroParagraph: From the canal-front backyards of Golden Isles to the high-rise pool decks near the Diplomat, a custom aluminum enclosure shields your Hallandale Beach pool from salt air, mosquitoes, and relentless sun — screened, shaded, and engineered to HVHZ 175 mph wind standards.
heroImageAlt: Aluminum pool screen enclosure over a backyard pool in Hallandale Beach, Florida with Intracoastal-area canal homes nearby
introHeading: Custom-Engineered Pool Cages Built for Hallandale Beach's Salt Air and Sun
valueParagraphs: Hallandale Beach sits between the Atlantic and the Intracoastal, and it shows in every backyard — from the canal-front docks of Golden Isles and Three Islands to the condo pool decks along Ocean Drive. Salt air and humidity eat through standard screen frames fast. Our pool enclosures use powder-coated, marine-grade aluminum built specifically for that exposure, so your cage keeps its finish and structural strength through Broward's harshest coastal seasons instead of pitting, chalking, or corroding within a few years.

A permanent pool cage in Hallandale Beach isn't a weekend add-on — Broward County is a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, so every structure needs a building permit, sealed engineering, and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval before installation begins. We handle that entire process, from the permit application with the city building department to final inspection, and design frames rated to withstand 175 mph winds. You also get a free 3D design of your exact pool and deck, an in-house crew for the build, and financing options.

A screened enclosure changes how you actually use a Hallandale Beach pool. It keeps out mosquitoes, no-see-ums, palm debris, and the occasional frog, while cutting down the leaf litter that drives up cleaning and chemical costs. Super-screen and no-see-um mesh options block biting insects without losing airflow or your view of the water, and the structure itself doubles as a safety barrier around the pool for kids and pets. Choose from mansard, dome, gable, or flat screen-roof styles, plus screen doors, to match your home's lines.

faqs.answer: For Hallandale Beach's salt air and heavy bug pressure, most homeowners choose 18x14 standard screen for airflow or upgrade to super-screen / no-see-um mesh for tighter protection against the tiny biting insects common near Golden Isles' canals. faqs.answer: If the aluminum frame is still structurally sound and meets current HVHZ wind standards, we can rescreen it with new mesh rather than rebuild. Older Hallandale Beach cages near the coast often need frame replacement too, since salt air corrodes fasteners and connectors faster than screen fabric wears out.

faqs.answer: A properly permitted aluminum enclosure with self-closing, self-latching screen doors can serve as part of your pool's required safety barrier under Florida's child-safety pool code, in addition to keeping out debris and insects. title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Fort Lauderdale
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Fort Lauderdale, FL
heroParagraph: Fort Lauderdale's canal-front lifestyle means salt air, mosquitoes, and relentless sun on pool decks from Rio Vista to Coral Ridge. Our marine-grade aluminum enclosures block bugs and UV, built to 175 mph HVHZ standards and permitted through the City.
heroImageAlt: Aluminum pool screen enclosure over a backyard pool in Fort Lauderdale, Florida
introHeading: Pool Screen Enclosures Built for Fort Lauderdale's Waterfront
valueParagraphs: Along Fort Lauderdale's finger-island canals in Rio Vista and Las Olas Isles, and up the Intracoastal in Coral Ridge, salt air corrodes ordinary screen frames within a few seasons. We build every enclosure from powder-coated marine-grade aluminum engineered for that salt exposure, so the frame holds its finish and strength year after year on the water. Screen options range from standard mesh up to super-screen and no-see-um weave, which matters here, since the bugs blowing off the canals get thick once the sun drops.

A screen enclosure changes how you actually use a pool. It keeps leaves, frogs, palmetto bugs, and wind-blown debris out of the water, cutting down on skimming, filter changes, and chemical costs. The mesh cuts glare and UV while still letting the breeze through, so the deck stays shaded and usable in the middle of the afternoon instead of scorching. For families in Victoria Park and Harbor Beach, the screen doors and full enclosure also add a real layer of separation between the yard and an unsupervised pool.

Every enclosure we build in Fort Lauderdale is engineered to the 175 mph HVHZ wind standard Broward requires, backed by Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval documentation and a sealed engineering package submitted to the City building department for permit. We handle that paperwork start to finish, plus a free 3D design so you can see the roof style, mansard, dome, gable, or flat screen, before we cut any aluminum. Our in-house crew installs the whole structure, and financing is available for the {range} project.
permitNote: Permanent pool enclosures in Fort Lauderdale require a building permit with sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval, rated to withstand 175 mph HVHZ winds. We prepare and submit the full package to the City of Fort Lauderdale building department (or Broward County, depending on location) and manage it through final inspection.
localProof: Licensed and insured with 15+ years building pool enclosures across Broward County and 500+ completed projects, from canal-front decks in Rio Vista to backyard pools further inland.
priceRangeNote: Most Fort Lauderdale pool enclosures fall within {range}, depending on size, roof style, and screen type; ask about our {perSqft} estimate for your specific pool deck.
answerQuestion: Will an aluminum pool enclosure hold up in a Fort Lauderdale hurricane?
answerAnswer: Yes, when it's built to code. Every enclosure we install in Broward is engineered to the HVHZ 175 mph wind standard, using Miami-Dade NOA-approved aluminum framing and screen fasteners, with a sealed engineering package permitted through the City. That's the same wind-load standard required for any permanent structure attached to a Fort Lauderdale home near the coast.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to install a pool enclosure in Fort Lauderdale?
faqs.answer: Yes. A permanent aluminum pool enclosure is a structural addition, so it requires a building permit, sealed engineering, and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval rated to 175 mph HVHZ winds. We prepare the drawings and submit the full package to the City of Fort Lauderdale building department and manage the process through final inspection.

faqs.question: What's the difference between standard screen and no-see-um mesh?
faqs.answer: Standard 18x14 screen blocks mosquitoes and larger insects and is the most common choice. No-see-um and super-screen mesh use a tighter weave that also stops the tiny biting midges common near Fort Lauderdale's canals, though it lets slightly less airflow through. Pet-screen is a heavier-gauge option for households with dogs or cats.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen my existing pool cage instead of replacing the whole enclosure?
faqs.answer: Often, yes. If the aluminum frame is sound and still meets current wind-load requirements, we can strip and rescreen it with your choice of mesh, which costs less than a full rebuild. If the frame has corrosion from salt air or storm damage, or predates current HVHZ engineering, a new enclosure is usually the safer, more cost-effective route.

faqs.question: Does a pool enclosure satisfy Broward's pool safety barrier requirements?
faqs.answer: A full screen enclosure with self-latching screen doors can serve as part of your pool's required safety barrier under Florida's pool safety act, which Broward enforces through the building permit process. We design door hardware and enclosure perimeter to meet that code, though your final inspection will confirm compliance for your specific property.

Un cerramiento de malla cambia cómo se usa realmente la piscina. Mantiene fuera hojas, ranas, insectos y escombros del viento, reduciendo la limpieza, los cambios de filtro y el gasto en químicos. La malla filtra el resplandor y los rayos UV sin bloquear la brisa, así que el área de la piscina se mantiene fresca a media tarde. faqsEs.answer: La malla estándar 18x14 bloquea mosquitos e insectos más grandes y es la opción más común. valueParagraphsEs: Parkland está tierra adentro, al noroeste de Broward, entre los campos de golf de Heron Bay y Parkland Golf & Country Club y las fincas de Cypress Head — y todos esos patios reciben el mismo sol implacable del verano. Un cerramiento de aluminio para piscina reduce la radiación UV directa sobre el agua y el deck mientras deja circular la brisa, así no tienes que elegir entre sombra y ventilación. También mantiene fuera lagartijas, hojas de palma y mosquitos de una piscina que ya no tendrás que limpiar constantemente.

Casi todas las comunidades cerradas de Parkland — Heron Bay, Parkland Golf & Country Club, Watercress — tienen su propio comité de revisión arquitectónica, y un cerramiento que no combine con el techo o los colores puede ser rechazado. Por eso hacemos un render 3D gratis, ajustado a techos tipo mansarda, domo, a dos aguas o plano, listo para tu aprobación. La misma estructura sirve de barrera de seguridad para niños y mascotas, con acabado en polvo de calidad marina resistente al sol de Florida.

Broward es zona de alta velocidad de huracán (HVHZ), así que toda estructura de aluminio permanente aquí necesita ingeniería sellada, aprobación NOA de Miami-Dade o Florida Product Approval, y diseño certificado para 175 mph antes de que la ciudad de Parkland emita el permiso — nosotros gestionamos ese proceso completo. Con más de 500 instalaciones en el sur de Florida, más de 15 años con licencia y seguro, y cuadrillas propias, ofrecemos malla estándar, no-see-um y super-screen, además de financiamiento.

title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Plantation
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Plantation, FL
heroParagraph: Plantation's warm, buggy evenings make an uncovered pool a losing battle against no-see-ums and falling jacaranda blooms. Our aluminum pool cages, engineered to Broward's 175 mph HVHZ standard, keep your pool shaded, screened, and swim-ready year-round. Free 3D design included.
heroImageAlt: Aluminum pool screen enclosure over a backyard pool in Plantation, FL
introHeading: Custom Pool Screen Enclosures Built for Plantation Homes
valueParagraphs: Plantation sits well inland from the coast, which means your pool deals less with salt spray and more with relentless subtropical sun and swarms of mosquitoes and no-see-ums drifting off the canals that thread through Jacaranda and Plantation Acres. A screened aluminum pool enclosure blocks that bug pressure while cutting harsh UV glare, so the water — and the deck around it — stays usable through the hottest stretch of a Florida afternoon without extra sunscreen or citronella.

Jacaranda and Fig Tree Island HOAs both run architectural review on anything visible from the street or golf course, and a pool cage is no exception — our free 3D design renders the exact roofline and screen-panel layout before you submit, so approval goes smoother the first time. Because Broward is a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, the City of Plantation Building Department requires sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval on every aluminum frame, rated to withstand 175 mph winds.

We've built and permitted pool cages across Broward for 15+ years and 500-plus projects, with our own in-house crew — no subcontractors — handling installation start to finish, and we manage the permit paperwork with the city for you. Choose standard 18x14 screen, no-see-um mesh, or pet-resistant super-screen, plus a mansard, dome, gable, or flat roofline and matching screen doors. Powder-coated marine-grade aluminum resists Florida's humidity, and financing options bring pricing to {perSqft} or a full-project {range}.

answerQuestion: Why do Plantation homeowners screen in their pools?
answerAnswer: Plantation's mix of golf-course canals, mature tree canopy, and thick summer bug season means an open pool collects leaves, jacaranda petals, and mosquitoes fast. A screened aluminum enclosure keeps the water and deck clean, cuts chemical and cleaning costs, tones down UV glare, and adds a safety layer around the pool for kids and pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit for a pool cage in Plantation?
faqs.answer: Yes — Broward is a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, so the City of Plantation Building Department requires sealed engineering plus Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval on the aluminum frame, rated for 175 mph winds; we handle every step of that permit process for you.

faqs.question: What screen mesh options are available?
faqs.answer: Standard 18x14 fiberglass screen for airflow and shade, no-see-um mesh with a finer weave for Broward's biting gnats, or pet-resistant super-screen for durability against claws and debris; we help you choose based on your yard's bug pressure and pets.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen my existing cage instead of building a new one?
faqs.answer: Yes — if the aluminum frame is structurally sound, we can rescreen panels and doors affordably. If the frame is undersized, corroded, or doesn't meet current HVHZ code, we'll recommend a new engineered cage instead.

faqs.question: Does a pool enclosure meet Florida's pool safety barrier requirements?
faqs.answer: A properly engineered aluminum cage with self-closing, self-latching screen doors can serve as a code-compliant safety barrier under Florida's pool safety act, adding a layer of protection for kids and pets in addition to keeping out debris.

We build every pool enclosure with powder-coated, marine-grade aluminum framing that shrugs off corrosion while sealing out no-see-ums, mosquitoes, and windblown debris that salt-air breezes carry onto your deck. The mesh cuts glare and UV without blocking the ocean breeze, and the enclosure doubles as a safety barrier around the pool. Screen doors keep access easy for family and guests.

Head west to Palm Aire or Cypress Bend and the challenge shifts from salt spray to sun and HOA scrutiny. Inland Pompano Beach summers push pool decks past comfortable temperatures, and our screen roofs — mansard, dome, gable, or flat — cut UV and heat while keeping the space breezy. Since HOA communities here require architectural approval before any structure goes up, we prepare the design package and permit submission for you. Fewer leaves and less sun exposure also mean less skimming and lower chemical costs.

Every permanent pool enclosure we install in Pompano Beach goes through the city building department (or Broward County for unincorporated parcels), backed by sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval — Broward sits inside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, so every frame is rated for 175 mph winds. Hacia el oeste, en Palm Aire o Cypress Bend, el reto cambia: ya no es la brisa salina, sino el sol y las normas de la HOA. Los veranos tierra adentro en Pompano Beach elevan rápido la temperatura del área de la piscina, y nuestros techos de malla —mansarda, domo, a dos aguas o plano— reducen el calor y los rayos UV mientras mantienen el espacio fresco y ventilado. title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Weston
valueParagraphs: Weston sits inland along the edge of the Everglades, where afternoon UV runs stronger and mosquito pressure off the wetlands never really lets up. A screened pool enclosure from AB Aluminum & Screens keeps your Weston Hills or Windmill Ranch backyard shaded and breezy while no-see-ums, palmetto bugs, and leaf litter stay outside the mesh. Powder-coated marine-grade aluminum framing holds up to daily sun without chalking or fading, and the screen cuts chlorine loss and skimmer debris so pool maintenance drops noticeably.

Nearly every community in Weston, from Country Isles to Savanna to Bonaventure, operates under an HOA architectural review board with its own rules on cage height, roof style, and screen color, on top of city permitting. Our in-house crew has pulled permits across Weston long enough to know which HOAs want a bronze frame, which allow white, and how to submit drawings that clear the design committee on the first pass, so your enclosure doesn't stall in review while the rest of your pool project waits.

Because Broward County sits inside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone, every permanent pool cage we build in Weston carries sealed engineering and Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval rated to 175 mph, filed with the City of Weston Building Department before a single post goes in the ground. Beyond storm performance, the enclosure adds a real safety barrier around the pool for kids and pets, with screen doors that self-close. Free 3D design and financing options make it easy to see the finished cage, and price it, before construction starts.
valueParagraphsEs: Weston está tierra adentro, al borde de los Everglades, donde el sol de la tarde pega más fuerte y la presión de mosquitos que viene de los humedales nunca baja del todo. Un cerramiento de piscina de AB Aluminum & Screens mantiene su patio en Weston Hills o Windmill Ranch fresco y con sombra, mientras los jejenes, cucarachas de palmera y hojas se quedan afuera de la malla. El marco de aluminio grado marino con pintura electrostática resiste el sol diario sin decolorarse, y la malla reduce la pérdida de cloro y la basura que cae en el skimmer, así que el mantenimiento de la piscina baja notablemente.

Casi toda comunidad en Weston, desde Country Isles hasta Savanna y Bonaventure, opera bajo una junta de revisión arquitectónica de la HOA con sus propias reglas sobre la altura del cerramiento, el estilo del techo y el color de la malla, además del permiso de la ciudad. Nuestro equipo interno lleva años tramitando permisos en Weston y sabe qué HOA prefiere marco bronce, cuál permite blanco, y cómo preparar los planos para que pasen la revisión del comité de diseño a la primera, sin que su cerramiento se quede detenido mientras el resto del proyecto de la piscina espera.

Como el condado de Broward está dentro de la Zona de Huracanes de Alta Velocidad (HVHZ), cada cerramiento permanente que construimos en Weston lleva ingeniería sellada y una Aprobación NOA de Miami-Dade o de Florida (Florida Product Approval) con clasificación de 175 mph, radicada ante el Departamento de Construcción de la Ciudad de Weston antes de instalar el primer poste. Además del desempeño ante tormentas, el cerramiento añade una barrera de seguridad real alrededor de la piscina para niños y mascotas, con puertas de malla que cierran solas. El diseño 3D gratuito y las opciones de financiamiento le permiten ver el cerramiento terminado, y su precio, antes de comenzar la obra.

title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Jupiter
valueParagraphs: Waterfront homes along Jupiter Island and Admirals Cove face constant salt spray off the Atlantic and Loxahatchee River, which corrodes ordinary screen frames within a few seasons. Our pool enclosures use powder-coated marine-grade aluminum engineered specifically for that exposure, so the frame resists pitting and the screen keeps out no-see-ums, mosquitoes and windblown debris without trapping the breeze off the water. A screened cage also gives young swimmers and pets a real barrier around the pool, not just a suggestion.

Inland in Abacoa and the neighborhoods off Central Boulevard, the challenge is different: long, unshaded afternoons that heat the deck and fade furniture, plus an HOA architectural review process before any structure goes up. We design each cage to your community's guidelines — flat, gable, dome or mansard screen roofs — and submit the paperwork ourselves. Once installed, the screen cuts direct UV while still moving air through the lanai, so the pool deck stays usable, and cooler, from midday through sunset.

Palm Beach County sits in the Wind-Borne Debris Region, with design wind speeds running roughly 165 to 175 mph near the coast, and any permanent pool enclosure here needs sealed engineering, Florida Product Approval and a permit through Palm Beach County PZB or the City of Jupiter. We build every cage to Miami-Dade HVHZ product standards — the toughest in Florida — so it clears local code easily. Choose standard, super-screen or pet-resistant mesh, add a screen door, and finance the project if you'd rather not pay all at once.
valueParagraphsEs: Las casas frente al agua en Jupiter Island y Admirals Cove reciben salitre constante del Atlántico y del río Loxahatchee, que corroe los marcos de malla comunes en pocas temporadas. Nuestros cerramientos usan aluminio marino con recubrimiento en polvo diseñado específicamente para esa exposición, por lo que el marco resiste la corrosión y la malla mantiene fuera a los jejenes, mosquitos y escombros arrastrados por el viento sin bloquear la brisa del agua. Una jaula de piscina también funciona como barrera real de seguridad para niños y mascotas.

Tierra adentro, en Abacoa y los vecindarios cerca de Central Boulevard, el reto es distinto: tardes largas y sin sombra que calientan el deck y decoloran los muebles, además de un proceso de revisión arquitectónica de la asociación de propietarios antes de instalar cualquier estructura. Diseñamos cada jaula según las normas de su comunidad — techos de malla planos, a dos aguas, domo o mansarda — y nosotros mismos tramitamos el papeleo. Una vez instalada, la malla reduce los rayos UV directos sin dejar de mover el aire por el lanai, así el área de la piscina se mantiene fresca y utilizable desde el mediodía hasta el atardecer.

El condado de Palm Beach está dentro de la Región de Escombros por Viento, con velocidades de diseño de aproximadamente 165 a 175 mph cerca de la costa, así que todo cerramiento de piscina permanente aquí necesita ingeniería sellada, Aprobación de Producto de Florida y un permiso tramitado ante Palm Beach County PZB o la ciudad de Jupiter. Construimos cada jaula bajo los estándares de producto HVHZ de Miami-Dade — los más exigentes del estado — para que su estructura cumpla el código local con margen de sobra. Elija malla estándar, super-screen o resistente a mascotas, agregue una puerta con malla, y financie el proyecto si prefiere no pagarlo todo de una vez.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit for a pool enclosure in Boca Raton?
faqs.answer: Yes. A permanent aluminum pool cage requires a building permit with sealed engineering and Florida Product Approval. Inside city limits that goes through the City of Boca Raton building division; in unincorporated Palm Beach County it's filed with PZB. We handle the entire permit process, engineered to Miami-Dade HVHZ standards that exceed what Palm Beach County requires.

faqs.question: What screen mesh options are available?
faqs.answer: Standard 18x14 fiberglass mesh balances airflow and shade for most pools. No-see-um and super-screen mesh block the tiny biting insects common near canals and preserve areas, and pet-screen is a heavier weave for homes with dogs or cats. We'll recommend the right mesh during your free 3D design.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen an existing cage instead of building new?
faqs.answer: Yes. If your frame is structurally sound, we can strip and rescreen it with fresh mesh and new spline, often for less than a full rebuild. If the aluminum is corroded — common in older salt-air-exposed enclosures near the Intracoastal — we'll recommend a new HVHZ-engineered structure instead.

faqs.question: How does a pool enclosure handle Boca Raton's storms and salt air?
faqs.answer: Our frames use powder-coated marine-grade aluminum and are engineered to Miami-Dade HVHZ product standards — built for design winds beyond what Palm Beach County's wind-borne debris region requires. That same marine-grade coating resists corrosion in salt-air neighborhoods near the Intracoastal and coastal canals.

faqsEs.question: ¿Necesito un permiso para un cerramiento de piscina en Boca Raton?
faqsEs.answer: Sí. Un cerramiento de aluminio permanente requiere un permiso de construcción con ingeniería sellada y aprobación de producto de Florida. Dentro de los límites de la ciudad se tramita con la división de construcción de la Ciudad de Boca Raton; en áreas no incorporadas de Palm Beach County se tramita con PZB. Nosotros manejamos todo el proceso de permisos, con ingeniería bajo los estándares HVHZ de Miami-Dade que superan lo que exige Palm Beach County.

faqsEs.question: ¿Qué opciones de malla tienen disponibles?
faqsEs.answer: La malla estándar 18x14 de fibra de vidrio equilibra ventilación y sombra para la mayoría de las piscinas. La malla no-see-um y super-screen bloquea los insectos diminutos comunes cerca de canales y zonas de preserva, y la malla para mascotas es un tejido más resistente para hogares con perros o gatos. Te recomendaremos la malla adecuada durante tu diseño 3D gratuito.

faqsEs.question: ¿Pueden volver a poner malla en una jaula existente en lugar de construir una nueva?
faqsEs.answer: Sí. Si tu estructura está en buen estado, podemos retirar y volver a instalar malla nueva con spline nuevo, generalmente por menos que una reconstrucción completa. Si el aluminio está corroído — algo común en cerramientos más antiguos expuestos al aire salino cerca del Intracoastal — te recomendaremos una estructura nueva con ingeniería HVHZ.

faqsEs.question: ¿Cómo resiste un cerramiento de piscina las tormentas y el aire salino de Boca Raton?
faqsEs.answer: Nuestros marcos usan aluminio marino con recubrimiento en polvo y están diseñados bajo los estándares HVHZ de Miami-Dade — construidos para vientos de diseño superiores a lo que exige la zona de escombros por viento de Palm Beach County. Ese mismo recubrimiento marino resiste la corrosión en vecindarios con aire salino cerca del Intracoastal y los canales costeros.

Combined with super-screen or no-see-um mesh, your cage keeps salt spray, storm debris, and biting insects out of the pool while letting the bay breeze through — without the pitting and rust that plague standard hardware near the water.

West of I-95 in neighborhoods like Lake Ida and the HOA communities around Rainberry Bay, the bigger issue is sun, not salt — and paperwork. A pool cage is also a safety barrier: it adds a real layer between an unattended pool and small kids or pets, on top of cutting the leaves, frogs, and lizards that turn into hours of extra skimming and chemical costs. Every enclosure we install is engineered and sealed to Miami-Dade HVHZ product standards — the toughest wind-load requirement in Florida — well above what Palm Beach County's wind-borne debris rules demand, so your structure is built for more storm than code strictly requires.

Palm Beach County sits in a Wind-Borne Debris Region, and we engineer every cage to Miami-Dade HVHZ standards — stricter than what's required here — then handle the permit paperwork and inspections for you.

faqs.question: What screen mesh options are available for a pool cage?
faqs.answer: Standard 18x14 fiberglass screen is the most common choice and blocks the vast majority of insects and debris. For yards near the Intracoastal or canals with heavier no-see-um pressure, we offer finer no-see-um mesh, plus super-screen or pet-screen options for households with dogs or cats that need a tougher weave.

faqs.answer: If your aluminum frame is structurally sound, we can rescreen it with fresh mesh and new spline for a fraction of the cost of a new enclosure. title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Royal Palm Beach
h1: Aluminum Pool Enclosures in Royal Palm Beach, FL
heroParagraph: Royal Palm Beach's inland heat and canal-side mosquitoes make bare pools miserable by afternoon. A custom aluminum enclosure from AB Aluminum & Screens keeps the bugs out, cuts the UV, and keeps swimming enjoyable all year — with a free 3D design to start.
heroImageAlt: Custom aluminum pool screen enclosure over a backyard pool in Royal Palm Beach, Florida
introHeading: Built for Royal Palm Beach's Inland Heat and Bugs
valueParagraphs: Royal Palm Beach sits well inland from the Intracoastal, so pool owners here fight a different battle than coastal neighbors: relentless midday sun and swarms of mosquitoes and no-see-ums breeding along the canals that run through Madison Green, Crestwood, and La Mancha. A pool screen enclosure from AB Aluminum & Screens blocks that UV without blocking the breeze, and seals out the bugs, leaves, and frogs that turn an open pool into a chore. Powder-coated marine-grade aluminum framing means the heat and humidity never get the last word.

Because a pool cage is a permanent structure, the Village of Royal Palm Beach Building Division requires a permit, sealed engineering, and Florida Product Approval before installation — Palm Beach County sits inside the state's Wind-Borne Debris Region, with design winds reaching roughly 165-175 mph closer to the coast. AB Aluminum & Screens engineers every enclosure to Miami-Dade HVHZ standards, the strictest product approval in Florida, so your cage clears Royal Palm Beach's requirements with margin to spare. We handle the permit paperwork and inspections start to finish.

Beyond comfort, an enclosure is a safety upgrade — screen walls and locking screen doors form a real barrier between the pool and small kids or pets in family-heavy neighborhoods like Madison Green and Crestwood. Fewer leaves and bugs in the water also means less skimming, less shock, and lower chemical costs month to month. Choose from mansard, dome, gable, or flat screen-roof styles, and upgrade to super-screen or no-see-um mesh where the canal-side bugs are worst. Our in-house crew installs it all, and financing is available.
permitNote: Any permanent aluminum pool enclosure in Royal Palm Beach needs a permit through the Village's Building Division, plus sealed structural engineering and Florida Product Approval — AB Aluminum & Screens handles all of it, building to Miami-Dade HVHZ standards that exceed what Palm Beach County requires.

faqs.answer: Yes. A permanent aluminum pool enclosure requires a building permit from the Village of Royal Palm Beach, plus sealed engineering and Florida Product Approval, since Palm Beach County sits in the state's Wind-Borne Debris Region. AB Aluminum & Screens prepares the engineering package and handles the permit process for you.

faqs.question: What screen mesh options are available for a Royal Palm Beach enclosure?
faqs.answer: Standard 18x14 mesh handles typical bugs and debris, super-screen or no-see-um mesh blocks the tiny biting insects common near Royal Palm Beach's canals and retention lakes, and pet-screen resists claws and impact. We help you pick the mix that fits your yard and budget.

faqs.question: Can you rescreen an existing pool cage instead of building new?
faqs.answer: If your aluminum frame is structurally sound, rescreening replaces torn or sun-worn mesh at a fraction of the cost of a new enclosure. We inspect the frame first — if the structure has failed or no longer meets current wind-load standards, we'll recommend a new HVHZ-rated cage instead.

faqs.question: Does a pool enclosure satisfy Florida's pool safety barrier law?
faqs.answer: Yes — a fully enclosed screen structure with self-latching screen doors can serve as the code-required barrier under Florida's residential swimming pool safety act, keeping young children and pets from unsupervised pool access. We build and position doors to meet those latch-height and enclosure requirements.

title: Aluminum Pool Enclosures — Lake Worth Beach
valueParagraphs: Lake Worth Beach sits right on the water — from the bungalows of College Park and Bryant Park to the canal lots backing up to the Lake Worth Lagoon, salt air is a daily reality for anything metal in your yard. We build every pool cage from powder-coated marine-grade aluminum specifically because standard hardware corrodes fast this close to the Intracoastal and the ocean at the Lake Worth Municipal Beach. Your frame stays rust-free and structurally sound for decades, not years.

A screen enclosure means you actually use your pool instead of skimming it every weekend. It keeps mosquitoes, no-see-ums, palmetto bugs and wind-blown leaves out of the water, cuts chlorine use and cleaning time, and blocks harsh South Florida UV so the deck stays shaded and swimmable in July. We offer standard 18/14 screen, no-see-um mesh for yards near the canals, and pet-resistant super-screen — plus mansard, dome, gable or flat roof styles and lockable screen doors that double as a safety barrier for kids and pets.

Palm Beach County sits in a Wind-Borne Debris Region, with design winds reaching roughly 165–175 mph near the coast — exactly the standard Lake Worth Beach falls under.
